MMM 2026 Values / CV:
MATERIAL: 
EVENTS: One of: Awards and prizegivings; Meeting and conference; Receptions and networking; Workshops and courses [default]
RDA Description: The predominant type or kind that characterizes the learning resource.
TeSS Description:
MATERIAL: The predominant category that describes the kind of learning material, for example, a presentation, video, handout, publication, poster.
EVENT: The category of an event based on its purpose, format, and activities.

RDA Description: The human readable name of the resource.
TeSS Description: A concise sentence that clearly indicates the topic of the training material. It should provide context and help learners understand what the material will cover.
Example 1 (Material): HemaFAIR Lecture 16 - Privacy-Preserving Record Linkage – Use Cases, Approaches, and Implementation Scenarios in Rare Diseases
Example 2 (Event): Causality in biomedicine: going beyond associations

RDA Description: URL that resolves to the learning resource or to a "landing page" for the resource that contains important contextual information including the direct resolvable link to the resource, if applicable.
TeSS Description:
MATERIAL: 
EVENT: Location where the event and its details are advertised on the internet.

RDA Description: A brief synopsis about or description of the learning resource.
TeSS Description: A short, informative summary that outlines the purpose, scope, key topic and of the training material. It should help the learners and instructors understand the content and relevance of the training before engaging with it.
Example 1 (Material): This HELIOS and HemaFAIR webinar provides an introduction to Privacy-Preserving Record Linkage (PPRL) and its application in rare disease and biomedical research. ...
Example 2 (Event): This course provides an introduction to causal inference and causal representation learning, offering both theoretical foundations and hands-on training. ...

Organization: Name, URL (ROR if exists)
RDA Description: Name of entity(ies) authoring the resource.
TeSS Description:
MATERIAL: Individuals who made substantial intellectual or technical contributions to the conception, design, execution of the training material. This field supports freetext name and ORCID.
EVENT: Institution/Organisation or the individual coordinating to organise the event.
PROPOSED: This field supports freetext name and ROR.

MMM 2026 Values / CV: Free text keywords are delimited by commas.
RDA Description: Keywords or tags used to describe the resource.
TeSS Description: Free text about topics clearly identifying the training material.
Example 1 (Material): FAIR principles, HemaFAIR, HELIOS, Open Science, Healthcare, Hemoglobinopathies, Rare Diseases, Privacy-Preserving, Record Linkage, Registries

RDA Description: Version date for the most recently published or broadcast resource.

MMM 2026 Values / CV:
Person: Name, URL (ORCID if exists).
Organization: Name, URL (ROR if exists)
TeSS Description:
MATERIAL: Individuals or entities who provided specific assistance, technical input, data, or materials to the work but may not meet the full criteria for authorship. This field supports freetext name and ORCID.

Example 1 (Material):
Petros Kountouris,  Sotiroula Chatzimatthaiou

RDA Description: A license document that applies to this content, typically indicated by URL
TeSS Description: The most appropriate license that specifies the terms under which your training material and dataset can be used, modified, and distributed. Various options of licenses are available to choose from, for example, Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

MMM 2026 Values / CV: URL of DOI or equivalent identifier
TeSS Description: A unique, persistent alphanumeric string assigned to the training material that provides a permanent link to its location for long-term accessibility and citation tracking.

RDA Description: Principal users(s) for which the resource was designed.
TeSS Description: The intended group of users or learners for whom the training material is designed, such as researchers, students, postdocs, trainers.
Example 1 (Material): Researchers, Data Scientist, Data stewards, Data managers, Students, Clinicians
Example 2 (Event): [PhD student, post-doctoral researcher, or research scientist currently working with clinical healthcare and/or molecular data]

MMM 2026 Values / CV: It is recommended that you utilize terms from the Blooms taxonomy
RDA Description: Descriptions of what knowledge, skills or abilities a learner should acquire on completion of the resource.
TeSS Description: Clearly defined, measurable statements describing the specific knowledge, skills a learner should acquire after completing the training course.
Example 1 (Material):
-Define Privacy-Preserving Record Linkage (PPRL) and its role in biomedical research.
-Compare different methods for privacy-preserving data linkage.
-Understand the challenges of linking sensitive health data while maintaining privacy and regulatory compliance.
-Identify opportunities for implementing PPRL in rare disease and clinical research settings.
-Appreciate the role of secure data integration in enabling collaborative and federated research initiatives.

Example from DReSA and DIRECT: Awareness, Working, Practitioner, Expert.
RDA Description: Target skill level in the topic being taught; example values include: beginner, intermediate, advanced.
TeSS Description: Gradations of expertise or proficiency expected of learners or users, categorised as beginner, intermediate, or advanced.

RDA Description: Designation of the identifier scheme used for the resource URL , e.g., DOI, ARK, Handle.

Access cost: Choice stating whether or not there is a fee for use of the resource (CV = Y/N/Maybe with recommendation that further explanation of “Maybe” goes in the Description field for “It depends” or “It changes” explanations).
TeSS Description: EVENT: Select the basis by which costs, if any, are applied. Select from: Free to all; Cost to non-members; Cost incurred by all

RDA Description: Language in which the resource was originally published or made available.
TeSS Description:
MATERIAL: 
EVENT: The language in which the training/seminar/lecture will be delivered. Select from the list of available options.

MMM 2026 Values / CV: Varies by instance, eg EDAM (ELIXIR), PaNET (PaNOSC).
TeSS Description: The scientific subject or field that the training material focuses on, for example, bioinformatics, genetics, biodiversity, genomics. In ELIXIR TeSS, this field uses the EDAM ontology. In PaN-Training, this field uses the PaNET ontology.
Example 1 (Material): Data protection, Data mining, Open science, FAIR data, Rare diseases
Example 2 (Event): Public health and epidemiology, Statistics and probability, Genomics, Systems biology

TeSS Description: The organisation, institution, or individual responsible for providing, and maintaining the training material.
